---
title: "project"
output: word_document
date: "2026-04-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

#Negative Binomial Test
library(MASS)
transmission<- read.csv("/Users/manyakaushik/Downloads/transmission_pairs.csv")
#head(transmission)

counts <- aggregate(infectee.case ~ infector.case,data = transmission,FUN = length)
colnames(counts)<- c("infector.case", "secondary_cases")

#negative binomial model
nb_model <- glm.nb(secondary_cases ~ 1, data = counts)
summary(nb_model)

#avg infected individual produces about 1.86 secondary cases 
exp((coef(nb_model)))


AIC(nb_model) 
#Dispersion parameter: theta= 7.24, suggesting that transmission not totally uniform, but transmission data is also not totally super spreading 









```
